title: Catherine Manager
description: Be the operator's first point of contact and run the work loop: understand the intent, delegate to the right specialists, integrate what they return, and drive each feature to done — keeping everything tracked in squads.
author: manager

extra.title: manager
extra.mission: Be the operator's first point of contact and run the work loop: understand the intent, delegate to the right specialists, integrate what they return, and drive each feature to done — keeping everything tracked in squads.
responsibilities: Triage incoming requests and clarify intent
Delegate work to the right specialist agents and integrate their results
Drive features through the loop (implement → review → fix) until done
Keep the backlog and statuses honest
extra.description: Default agent: triages the operator's request and routes it to the right specialist.

title: Robert Architect
description: Own the system's shape: design coherent solutions, record decisions as ADRs, and guide implementation.
author: architect

extra.title: architect
extra.mission: Own the system's shape: design coherent solutions, record decisions as ADRs, and guide implementation.
responsibilities: Record significant design decisions (ADRs in the bundled workflow)

title: Pierre Chat
author: op-pierre

title: sq check flags operator authors/assignees as unregistered
description: check() validates author/assignee against roles only, while the write gate accepts operators too

title: Road to 1.0
description: Define and prove the 1.0 stability promise across our public surfaces
author: product-owner

title: Stability contract documentation
description: docs/stability.md + README tiering the public surfaces and what each promises through 1.0
author: product-owner

subentities.title: Migration promise for 0.x squads reaching 1.0
SemVer-stable CLI and --json surfaces documented
Internals explicitly marked non-public

title: Project-level template and role overrides
description: squads/.templates/ overrides; lookup path and precedence join the durable contract, so it must land before 1.0
author: product-owner

subentities.title: Override item templates from squads/.templates/
As a project lead, I want to add or override role definitions for my project, so that the squad matches my actual team
Defined precedence and staleness behaviour for overrides
Supply agent names at init and role creation

title: Machine-readable surface audit
description: --json on every read command, documented exit codes, golden-file tests freezing the JSON shapes
author: product-owner

subentities.title: --json on every read command including blocked, mine, workload, inbox
As a CI pipeline author, I want documented exit codes, so that I can gate builds on commands like sq check
As a tool builder, I want the JSON shapes frozen by tests, so that an sq upgrade can't break my parser unannounced

title: Second backend: generic AGENTS.md
description: Prove the AgentBackend ABC with a cross-tool AGENTS.md implementation before the ABC freezes
author: product-owner

subentities.title: AGENTS.md backend for non-Claude agent tooling
Backend conformance test suite for AgentBackend implementers

title: Slug arguments not validated: sq mine accepts unknown agents and silently defaults to manager
description: sq mine/inbox exit 0 for unknown slugs and bare sq mine defaults to manager; slug args should be required and validated against the roster across commands
author: op-pierre

title: Counter can regress, allowing sequence-number reuse after an item file disappears
description: Hand-edited or repair-rebuilt indexes accept a regressed counter, so deleted items' numbers get reused; repair sets counter to max-found, ignoring the previous high-water mark
author: product-owner

title: show prints a redundant 'Body' literal before the body
description: sq <type> <n> show prefixes the body with a bare 'Body' label; the body is self-evident after the metadata panel and usually opens with its own headings
author: op-pierre

title: sq ui — a terminal UI for browsing the squad
description: An sq ui command opening a TUI: navigate the tree, read items with tabs for stories/body/discussion; browse-first, mutations a later increment
author: product-owner

title: sq web — a local web view of the squad
description: An sq web command starting a local webserver to browse the squad in a browser; read-only first, with full CRUD as a possible long-term destination
author: product-owner

title: Squads backend
description: Umbrella for technical work on the sq engine itself — services, index, models, workflow, rendering, agent-backend plumbing — as opposed to product surfaces like the CLI grammar, TUI or web
author: product-owner

title: Pluggable index store: SQLite / SQL databases behind the index
description: Abstract the index behind a store interface and offer SQLite (and real databases via SQLAlchemy) as alternatives to .squads.json — the .md files stay the only source of truth
author: product-owner

subentities.title: Real transactions for parallel sq calls without global file lock
Indexed store keeps list/tree/search fast at thousands of items
Store switching via config plus sq repair, reversible both ways

title: Remote mode: the sq CLI as a client to a squads server
description: A mode switch in .squads.toml (offline default / remote): in remote mode every command calls remote endpoints with full feature-and-flag parity, through a Protocol-typed service interface the CLI depends on
author: product-owner

subentities.title: CLI decoupled from concrete Service via Python Protocols
Full offline/remote parity: transport never changes how I work
Remote mode opt-in via .squads.toml; offline is the default

title: CLI frontend
description: The daily terminal experience of sq: complete and rendered reading, graph navigation, and the ergonomics of driving a squad from the command line
author: product-owner

title: Fix counter regression and sequence-number reuse (BUG-000022)
author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Repair keeps high-water mark: counter = max(previous counter, max found on disk)
Load-time counter validation: repair upward when stored counter is below max item sequence
Report items missing from disk vs the previous index in repair/check
Regression tests (service + CLI) covering both regression routes

subentities.title: Add resolve_slug_or_raise helper to _cli/_common.py

subentities.title: Function-local Status import in _check_decisions; hoist to module top

title: Review: FEAT-000026 rendered show output

subentities.title: Plain-output escaping leak: sub-entity pane titles double-escaped in piped/NO_COLOR/--raw path
Nit: badge/column duplication between _common.py and _discussion.py
Nit: test gap — no assertion for literal-bracket fidelity in plain pane titles

title: FEAT-000064: negative-path error UX leaks the internal _addr subgroup and fails 'error comprehensibly'
description: AddressDispatchGroup routes every unknown token to the hidden _addr subgroup; removed/typo'd/old-grammar invocations produce baffling errors that leak _addr and omit the documented migration hint.
author: reviewer

subentities.title: Removed list commands do not error comprehensibly (US3 acceptance)
The hidden _addr name leaks into every negative-path usage/error line
show/regen/rm are undiscoverable from sq role / --help (US1 spirit)
N1 slug shadowing: slug matching a group verb is unaddressable by slug
N2 resolver duplication: resolve_agent_addr iterates db.items inline instead of reusing item helpers
N3 empty-body hint inaccurate: suggests a 'body' verb the groups do not have
N4 inline shape check: _resolve_addr reimplements full-ID detection instead of _is_full_id_shape
N5 stale comment: SKIL-000002 should be SKILL-000002 in test_skill_item_first_grammar
N6 test coverage: no direct test for REV-000061 bracket/no-backslash fidelity on the new body path
N7 sq dev consistency: still uses the old listing shape, reads inconsistently against the flipped groups

title: Frontmatter is the source of truth; the index is rebuildable
description: Item state lives in .md frontmatter; .squads.json is a derived index that sq repair reconstructs
author: architect

title: One global monotonic ID counter across all item types
description: A single counter makes every ID number globally unique; allocation only inside the index transaction
author: architect

title: Forward-only refs with computed backrefs
description: Items store outgoing refs with kind inline; backrefs are computed by inversion, never persisted
author: architect

title: squads architecture: layering, data model, and the marker mechanism
description: A standalone-readable map of how squads is built: the cli to services to index/backends/rendering layering, the item/sub-entity/index data model, and the marker mechanism — pointing at sq docs internals for depth and citing the standing ADRs.
author: architect

title: Read-only browse: sidebar tree + rendered preview
description: VS Code activity-bar tree from sq tree --json; clicking an item opens sq show --raw (clean markdown) in the markdown preview; filter/group + refresh
author: product-owner

subentities.title: Squad hierarchy in VS Code sidebar with status and blocked-state
Clicking a tree node opens sq show --raw in markdown preview
Filter and group tree by type/state, refresh on demand

title: Exclusive work leases / claims
author: product-owner

subentities.title: Clear conflict error on concurrent item mutation
Manager visibility into all active leases

title: Parameterized item-file storage: prefix scope vs layout, and the ItemStore locator seam
description: Global prefix changes the ID prefix only; folder layout is a separate knob; both plug into a shared ItemStore locator
author: architect

title: Format-neutral item-file storage contract: codec seam + named body/prose regions
description: Split the store into a format-agnostic locator and a pluggable codec; define body regions as named fields, not text spans, so the two invariants hold across markdown/JSON/XML
author: architect
